prompt:
You are an engineering experiment design assistant. Return JSON only.

Task:
{
  "task_id": "task_demo_002",
  "paper_id": "paper_demo_002",
  "domain": "additive_manufacturing",
  "task_type": "experiment_design",
  "goal": "Design one feasible experiment to study how laser power affects porosity in a DED NiTi process.",
  "available_resources": {
    "equipment": [
      "DED machine",
      "SEM",
      "XRD",
      "precision saw",
      "mounting press",
      "grinding and polishing kit"
    ],
    "materials": [
      "NiTi powder",
      "argon gas",
      "mounting resin"
    ],
    "constraints": [
      "Use only listed equipment and materials.",
      "Use explicit numeric laser power levels.",
      "Keep all non-power settings fixed."
    ]
  },
  "evidence_chunk_ids": [
    "paper_002_chunk_01"
  ],
  "evidence_summary": "The process uses a DED machine with argon shielding gas. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging. SEM and XRD are used for characterization. A previously validated stable operating window is available for selecting feasible laser power levels.",
  "evidence": [
    {
      "source_id": "paper_002_chunk_01",
      "text": "The process uses a DED machine with argon shielding gas. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging. SEM and XRD are used for characterization. A previously validated stable operating window is available for selecting feasible laser power levels."
    }
  ]
}
chosen: {"goal": "Design one feasible experiment to study how laser power affects porosity in a DED NiTi process.", "hypothesis": "Within the previously validated operating window, changing laser power will change porosity.", "resources_used": ["DED machine", "SEM", "precision saw", "mounting press", "grinding and polishing kit", "NiTi powder", "argon gas", "mounting resin"], "independent_variables": ["laser power"], "dependent_variables": ["porosity"], "controls": ["Keep all non-laser power settings fixed across all conditions.", "Use the same NiTi powder lot, argon shielding setup, and sample geometry for every run.", "Prepare every sample with the same sectioning, mounting, grinding, and polishing workflow before SEM imaging."], "design": {"conditions": ["Laser power = 90% of the validated midpoint setpoint", "Laser power = 100% of the validated midpoint setpoint", "Laser power = 110% of the validated midpoint setpoint"], "replicates": 3, "procedure_outline": ["Run one DED build at each explicit numeric laser power condition within the previously validated stable operating window.", "Keep the same powder lot, shielding setup, geometry, and all non-target process settings across conditions.", "Section each build with the precision saw, then mount, grind, and polish for cross-section imaging.", "Acquire SEM cross-sections for every replicate and record the porosity measurement for each sample.", "Use XRD only as a secondary sanity check if phase consistency needs confirmation."]}, "measurement_plan": ["Quantify porosity from SEM cross-sections for every replicate using one consistent image-analysis workflow.", "Record the exact numeric laser power level and replicate identifier for every sample.", "Summarize porosity by condition before comparing the low, midpoint, and high settings."], "analysis_plan": ["Compute the mean and spread of porosity for each numeric laser power level.", "Plot porosity versus laser power to check for a monotonic or non-linear trend.", "Use the first-pass result to decide whether a narrower follow-up sweep is justified."], "feasibility_checks": ["Uses only listed equipment and materials.", "Uses three explicit numeric laser power levels inside the previously validated stable operating window.", "Keeps all non-target settings fixed and includes replicated SEM-based measurements."], "evidence_used": ["paper_002_chunk_01"]}
